CisMapping.description:
CIS Controls mapping entry.

Stub type — structured safeguard fields can be added in a future pass.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the safeguard and how the implementation addresses it.

ComponentType.description: Component type in a control implementation.

oneOf.description: US Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act.
Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard.
Catalog not covered by a specific variant. Prefer a specific variant when possible.

name.description: Human-readable identifier of the component (e.g. "Privacy Policy", "Datadog").

role.description: How this component contributes to the control — one sentence explaining its role.

type.description: Component category aligned with OSCAL component definition model.

path.description:
Location of the evidence artifact. Accepts (in order of preference):
a CorpoSpec PathRef (e.g. `legal/policies/privacy-policy`), a repository-relative
filesystem path (e.g. `legal/policies/privacy-policy.md`), a URI, or a content hash.

GdprMapping.description:
GDPR article mapping entry.

Stub type — prefer adding structured fields (article, paragraph, subject-right) in a
future pass once GDPR mappings are authored in real YAML.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the article and how the implementation addresses it.
Human-readable summary of the control and how the implementation addresses it.

NistCsfMapping.description:
NIST Cybersecurity Framework mapping entry.

Stub type — structured subcategory fields can be added in a future pass.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the subcategory and how the implementation addresses it.

PathRef.description:
Path-based cross-reference relative to .corpospec/ root.
Pattern: `^[a-z0-9_-]+(/[a-z0-9_.-]+)+$`

description.description: Human-readable summary of the criterion and how this control addresses it.

description: Security control implementation aligned with OSCAL component definition model.

assessor.description:
Party that assessed the control. Accepts a CorpoSpec PathRef into `people/**`
(e.g. `people/team/maik`) or an RFC 5321 email address for external assessors.
Required for audit trail — omit only for self-attestations that have not yet been reviewed.

catalog.description: Catalog that defines `control_id`. Use a specific variant when available.

control_id.description:
Catalog-specific control identifier. The expected format depends on `catalog`:
ISO 27001 uses `A.X.Y` (e.g. `A.9.1.1`), SOC 2 uses `CCx.y` (e.g. `CC6.1`),
NIST 800-53 uses `AC-1` etc. Free-form string to accommodate any catalog.

gdpr_mapping.description: GDPR article mappings for controls that address data protection obligations.

implementation.description:
Description of how the control is implemented plus the components that realise it.
Controls without an `implementation` are incomplete; populate this once the control
moves beyond `planned`.

title.description: Short human-readable title of the control (e.g. "Access Control Policy").

title: SecurityControl
